methods.name: Stage-Progression

methods.name: Peer-Median

data.narrative:
H Company is a Series D AI & Software company founded by Laurent Sifre, Charles Kantor, Daan Wiestra, Karl Tuyls, and Julien Perollat, having raised $220M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of 5 same-sector, same-stage private comparables (Wayve, Cohere, Hugging Face, Hive AI, Labelbox) alongside 1 public reference point, spanning a market range of $1.0B to $8.6B.

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-Progression modeling, using total raised as a proxy base of $5.5B and applying a typical Series D step-up multiplier of 1.5x, implies a valuation of $8.2B. Peer-Median analysis of the 5-company cohort yields a median valuation of $4.5B, with the peer set dispersed between $1.0B and $8.6B. These methods reflect materially different anchors: the former assumes substantial capital-multiple expansion; the latter reflects actual market-clearing prices for comparable-stage, sector-matched businesses.

Triangulating both methods produces a range of $3.8B (low) to $9.5B (high), with a median implied valuation of $6.4B. The wide spread reflects genuine uncertainty in how much premium H Company commands relative to the peer median, and whether stage-typical step-ups apply given the heterogeneity within Series D AI funding. Key unknowns include company-specific unit economics, revenue scale, and burn rate relative to peers.
